private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Swarm Aero is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers—including Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Stark Defence ($500M), and Scale AI ($29.0B)—alongside broader public market reference points in the sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement, yielding $20M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable firms produces a $300M implied valuation; the peer set ranges from $60M (floor) to $29.0B (ceiling), reflecting heterogeneity in scope and capital efficiency across defense tech. The wide range reflects meaningful dispersion in the comp set, particularly the presence of Scale AI as an outlier.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $17M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $160M. The stage-progression method anchors toward the lower bound, while peer-median pulls substantially higher. Key uncertainties include: differentiation relative to well-capitalized Series A peers; the relevance of Scale AI as a comp given its substantial outlier status; and burn trajectory relative to the $8M raised. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty about Swarm Aero's position within the defense-tech ecosystem at Seed stage.
